llm_pattern: The absolute current values decrease exponentially as voltage approaches zero from both negative and positive sides, spanning many orders of magnitude. The current magnitude ranges from approximately 2.4E-5 A at -6 V to about 2.2E-5 A at 6 V, with a minimum near zero voltage on the order of 1E-103 A. The reciprocal of the absolute voltage increases as voltage magnitude decreases. The natural logarithm of the ratio of absolute current to voltage squared decreases monotonically with decreasing voltage magnitude, reaching very large negative values near zero voltage. The device area and thickness remain constant throughout the dataset.

llm_keywords.evidence: Current magnitude decreases from about 2.4E-5 A at -6 V to approximately 6.6E-15 A at -1 V and further down to 8.9E-103 A near 0 V.
Current values at positive voltages mirror those at corresponding negative voltages, e.g., 2.4E-5 A at -6 V and 2.2E-5 A at 6 V.
The ln(|I|/V^2) values decrease from about -14 at high voltage magnitudes to approximately -230 near zero voltage.
Device_area is consistently 20 um^2 and Device_thickness is consistently 2 nm across all data points.
